name: Pluggable 'Effect' Handlers

userStory.iWant: to register custom side-effect handlers for network, database, or git operations
userStory.soThat: my application logic remains pure and 100% testable via mock ports.

acceptanceCriteria: The BijouContext supports a dynamic 'Ports' registry
Users can define custom Ports (e.g. HttpPort, GitPort)
A Cmd can request a specific Port from the context during execution
Standardized mock implementations exist for all built-in 'Effect' ports
backwardCompatibility: Current Cmds that use direct imports or global fetch will continue to work, but will be discouraged in documentation in favor of port-based effects.

inScope: Cmd execution bridge for dynamic ports
Built-in prototypes for HttpPort and FilePort
outScope: Implementing the actual heavy logic for every possible third-party API (just providing the interface/adapter pattern)

testPlan.goldenPath: Define an App that uses an HttpPort to fetch data. In tests, provide a MockHttpPort that returns a static string. Assert that the App correctly updates with the mocked data without making a real network call.
edgeCases: Requesting a Port that hasn't been registered in the context
Port implementations that return incorrect types
Concurrent port access from multiple Cmds
testPlan.fuzzStress: Register 1000 unique mock ports and verify that the context resolution remains O(1).
failureCases: Port registration collision (two handlers for the same interface)
